Class Goal

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.proof.Goal
All Implemented Interfaces:
ProofGoal<Goal>
public final class Goal extends Object implements ProofGoal<Goal>
A proof is represented as a tree of nodes containing sequents. The initial proof consists of just one node -- the root -- that has to be proved. Therefore it is divided up into several sub goals and so on. A single goal is not divided into sub goals any longer if the contained sequent becomes an axiom. A proof is closed if all leaves are closed. As the calculus works only on the leaves of a tree, the goals are the additional information needed for the proof is only stored at the leaves (saves memory) and not in the inner nodes. This class represents now a goal of the proof, this means a leave whose sequent is not closed. It keeps track of all applied rule applications on the branch and of the corresponding rule application index. Furthermore it offers methods for setting back several proof steps. The sequent has to be changed using the methods of Goal.

  • Method Details

    • hasApplicableRules

      public static boolean hasApplicableRules(Goal goal)
      Checks if the Goal has applicable rules.
      Parameters:
      goal - The Goal to check.
      Returns:
      true has applicable rules, false no rules are applicable.

      this object manages the tags for all formulas of the sequent

      Returns:
      the strategy that determines automated rule applications for this goal

      public @Nullable RuleApplicationManager<Goal> getRuleAppManager()
      Description copied from interface: ProofGoal
      returns the prover component responsible for providing the next rule application to be applied on this goal
      Specified by:
      getRuleAppManager in interface ProofGoal<Goal>
      Returns:
      the RuleApplicationManager selecting the next rule application to be applied on this goal

    • getLocalNamespaces

      public NamespaceSet getLocalNamespaces()
      returns the namespaces for this goal.
      Returns:
      an up-to-date non-null namespaces-set.

    • addGoalListener

      public void addGoalListener(GoalListener l)
      adds the listener l to the list of goal listeners. Attention: A listener added to this goal will be taken over when splitting into subgoals.
      Parameters:
      l - the GoalListener to be added

    • removeGoalListener

      public void removeGoalListener(GoalListener l)
      removes the listener l from the list of goal listeners. Attention: The listener is just removed from 'this' goal not from the other goals. (All goals can be accessed via proof openGoals())
      Parameters:
      l - the GoalListener to be removed

    • ruleAppIndex

      public RuleAppIndex ruleAppIndex()
      returns the index of possible rule applications at this node

    • indexOfTaclets

      public TacletIndex indexOfTaclets()
      returns the Taclet index for this goal. This is just a shortcut to the Taclet index of the RuleAppIndex
      Returns:
      the Taclet index assigned to this goal

    • appliedRuleApps

      public ImmutableList<RuleApp> appliedRuleApps()
      returns set of rules applied at this branch
      Returns:
      IList applied rule applications

    • getTime

      public long getTime()
      Description copied from interface: ProofGoal
      returns the current time of this goal the only requirement is that a goal that is a successor of this goal returns a larger number
      Specified by:
      getTime in interface ProofGoal<Goal>
      Returns:
      the current time of this goal (which is just the number of applied rules)

      Checks if is an automatic goal.
      Returns:
      true, if is automatic

    • setEnabled

      public void setEnabled(boolean t)
      Sets the automatic status of this goal.
      Parameters:
      t - the new status: true for automatic, false for interactive

    • isLinked

      public boolean isLinked()
      Checks if is this node is linked to another node (for example due to a MergeRule).
      Returns:
      true iff this goal is linked to another node.

    • setLinkedGoal

      public void setLinkedGoal(Goal linkedGoal)
      Sets the node that this goal is linked to; also sets this for all parents.

      TODO: Check whether it is problematic when multiple child nodes of a node are linked; in this case, the linkedNode field would be overwritten.

      Parameters:
      linkedGoal - The goal that this goal is linked to.

    • setSequent

      public void setSequent(SequentChangeInfo sci)
      sets the sequent of the node
      Parameters:
      sci - SequentChangeInfo containing the sequent to be set and describing the applied changes to the sequent of the node currently pointed to by this goal

    • addFormula

      public void addFormula(SequentFormula cf, PosInOccurrence p)
      adds a formula to the sequent before the given position and informs the rule application index about this change
      Parameters:
      cf - the SequentFormula to be added
      p - PosInOccurrence encodes the position

    • addFormula

      public void addFormula(SequentFormula cf, boolean inAntec, boolean first)
      adds a formula to the antecedent or succedent of a sequent. Either at its front or back and informs the rule application index about this change
      Parameters:
      cf - the SequentFormula to be added
      inAntec - boolean true(false) if SequentFormula has to be added to antecedent (succedent)
      first - boolean true if at the front, if false then cf is added at the back

    • changeFormula

      public void changeFormula(SequentFormula cf, PosInOccurrence p)
      replaces a formula at the given position and informs the rule application index about this change
      Parameters:
      cf - the SequentFormula replacing the old one
      p - the PosInOccurrence encoding the position

    • removeFormula

      public void removeFormula(PosInOccurrence p)
      removes a formula at the given position from the sequent and informs the rule appliccation index about this change
      Parameters:
      p - PosInOccurrence encodes the position

      public void addTaclet(Taclet rule, SVInstantiations insts, boolean isAxiom)

      rule - the Taclet of the TacletApp to create
      insts - the given instantiations of the TacletApp to be created

    • clone

      public Goal clone(Node node)
      clones the goal (with copy of tacletindex and ruleAppIndex).

      The local symbols are reused. This is taken care of later.

      Parameters:
      node - the new Node to which the goal is attached
      Returns:
      Object the clone

    • copy

      public Goal copy() throws CloneNotSupportedException

      Returns:

      Throws:
      CloneNotSupportedException

      Parameters:
      app - the applied rule app

    • removeLastAppliedRuleApp

      public void removeLastAppliedRuleApp()
      PRECONDITION: appliedRuleApps.size () > 0

    • split

      public ImmutableList<Goal> split(int n)
      creates n new nodes as children of the referenced node and new n goals that have references to these new nodes.
      Parameters:
      n - number of goals to create
      Returns:
      the list of new created goals.

    • splitAndTransform

      public ImmutableList<Goal> splitAndTransform(List<@Nullable Consumer<Goal>> funcs)
      Creates new nodes as children of the referenced node and apply each given non-null goal transformer to each proof.
      Returns:
      the list of new created goals, manipulated by funcs

    • setBranchLabel

      public void setBranchLabel(String s)

    • apply

      public ImmutableList<Goal> apply(RuleApp ruleApp)
      Perform the provided rule application on this goal. Returns the new goal(s), if any. This will also populate a RuleAppInfo object and fire the corresponding event. The state of the proof is also updated.
      Specified by:
      apply in interface ProofGoal<Goal>
      Parameters:
      ruleApp - the rule app
      Returns:
      new goal(s)

      public Services getOverlayServices()

    • makeLocalNamespacesFrom

      public void makeLocalNamespacesFrom(NamespaceSet ns)
      Update the local namespaces from a namespace set.

      The parameter is copied and stored locally.

      Parameters:
      ns - a non-null set of namesspaces which applies to this goal.
